resources.string: This translation was created by an automatic translator. You can improve it by using our collective translation service.

__address__ = A20
__mask__ FFFFFFFF

__address__ != B20
__mask__ FF0

__address__ = 0B?0
__mask__ FFF

__address__ != ??F??
__mask__ BA0

__rtfm__
Parallel Space, VirtualXposed, Parallel Space Lite, GO multiple, 2Face

:
	* Nox - https://www.bignox.com/
	* Memu - http://www.memuplay.com/
	* LDPlayer - http://en.ldmnq.com
	* KoPlayer - http://www.koplayer.com/
	* Droid4X - http://droid4x.haimawan.com/

__text__ UTF-8:
	:G
	:GG
	:gold
	:__text__ UTF-8

__text__ UTF-16LE:
	;Z
	;ZZ
	;sand
	;__text__ UTF-16LE

__hex_input__:
	h DF 59 37 5F 00

__hex_input__ + UTF-8 + UTF-16LE:
	Q DF 59 '__text__ UTF-8' 37 5F "__text__ UTF-16LE" 48 5E 00
	Q DF 59 '__text__ UTF-8' 37 5F 00
	Q DF 59 "__text__ UTF-16LE" 48 5E 00
	Q DF 59 37 5F 00

':' - UTF-8
	';' - UTF-16LE
	'h' - HEX
	'Q' - HEX + UTF-8 + UTF-16LE
Virtual Xposed, App Hider, Phone, Calculator, Dual App, Notepad, Multiple Accounts
If you have any questions about __app_name__ or its use, please visit our forums at __forum__
Found an old version of __app_name__. Do you want to uninstall it?
Failed to find the application package name!
Set a the path for temp files, e.g. \'__sdcard__\'. It may require a few hundred MB of free space. Current search results will be lost.
Failed to run daemon. Is root installed?
Started collection of data to write to the region log...
Do you want to remove this item?
Unhandled exception. Send the below info to the development team?
Add to value, do not replace
Could not attach to process (PTRACE_ATTACH): Operation not permitted

You loaded a list that has been saved from another instance of the game. Most likely, all of the addresses in the memory have changed and loaded data that does not correspond to data that was previously saved. We recommend you check these.
__app_name__ needs to be restarted in order to apply security permissions.
Out of memory error. Current result list cleared. Please set a smaller value for the number of results to show.
Number \'__s__\' is less than minimum value (__d__) for this type.
Number \'__s__\' is greater than maximum value (__d__) for this type.

If you want to help with or do a translation of the application, you can contact us on the forum http://gameguardian.net/forum, to get your translation included in the official release of the application.
__app_name__ needs to be restarted to apply changes.
Nothing found. Try to search again.
You can also try to choose more ranges in the settings tab.
You can also try to search for an unknown value.

To search for a known value, press "__search_known_value__".
If the value is unknown or encrypted - click the "__search_unknown_value__" to search.
Search for float values by their integer part may be performed by selecting the '__type_auto__' search type.
Also you can use a group search with '__semicolon__' as a separator.
Range end (\'__s__\') must be greater than \'__s__\'.
The application can not work normally because the system service was stopped. Try restarting the application or reboot the device.
Enter a value to search for
Select the type of data to search
Select the data type to change
This function is not available if daemon is not running. Wait until daemon starts up. (Did you forget to grant root access? Or is root not installed?)
Failed to copy text to the clipboard:
To change the speed of the game, perform a long press on the floating __app_name__ icon.
Your firmware has errors that prevented the use of this feature.
Please enable permissions on the next screen.
Speedhack for this process is not supported
You can open speedhack by long pressing on the floating __app_name__ icon.
Group size must be between __d__ and __d__:
For group search there must be at least two numbers

* The floating icon:
	*	Tap to open the interface.
	*	Tap and hold to toggle the speedhack panel.
	*	It can be moved to any place by dragging and dropping the icon.
	*	Transparency and hide timer can be customized in app settings.

* Exact search:
	1. Switch to __app_name__ from a game.
	2. Tap the "__search_known_value__" button in the "__search__" tab.
	3. Input the number that is displayed in the game.
	4. Select the suitable data type, or choose "__type_auto__" (if you are not sure) and other options (if needed) and click "__search__".
	5. When finished searching, search results will be displayed in the list.
	6. Go back to the game. After the value has changed, switch to __app_name__ again.
	7. Search for the value that changed.
	8. If there are too many results, continue to step 6.
	9. If only a few results are left, you can try to modify, store or freeze the value.

* Speedhack:
	*	Tap and hold on the floating icon to show the speedhack panel.
	*	Tap the arrows on either side of the icon, to change the game speed.
	*	Tap and hold the current speed number to reset the game speed.
	*	Tap and hold the floating icon to hide the speedhack panel.
	*	List of speeds and timers used can be customized in app settings.
	*	If the game does not respond to input or speedhack does not work, try using other green timers in __app_name__ settings.
	*	You can use "__time_jump__" to jump ahead in time. Just select the menu on the search tab and choose "__time_jump__". You will need to specify how many seconds to jump. Amount to jump can be fractional.

* Fuzzy search:
	1. Switch to __app_name__ from a game.
	2. Tap the "__search_unknown_value__" button in the "__search__" tab.
	3. Select the suitable data type, or choose "__type_auto__" (if you are not sure) and click "__search__".
	4. When finished searching, search results will be displayed in the list.
	5. Back to the game. After the value changed (or not), switch to __app_name__ again.
	6. Tap the "__search_unknown_value__" button and select the type for how the value has changed.
	7. If there are too many results, continue step to 5.
	8. If only a few results are left, you can try to modify, store or freeze the value.

	You can specify the exact change of the value by clicking the "__more__" button.
	You can enter a range as an accurate value change:
		190__tilde__210
	To specify change outside the range of values, you can input:
		10__tilde____tilde__4000

* Group search:
	1. Switch to __app_name__ from a game.
	2. Tap the "__search_known_value__" button in the "__search__" tab.
	3. Input search request for group search.
		Format: value 1 __semicolon__ value 2 __semicolon__ ... __semicolon__ value N __colon__ group size
		2-__max_group__ values can be searched at the same time. The default group size is 512. The group size must be between 2 and 65536.
		For example, if you want to search 195 and 6543, you should input:
			195 __semicolon__ 6543
		Of course you can specify the group size (300):
			195 __semicolon__ 6543 __colon__ 300
		Also, you can set one or more data types:
			195__type_dword_short__ __semicolon__ 6543
		This means you want to search a __type_dword__ value that is 195 and an __type_auto__ value that is 6543.
		Possible data types are the same as they are in search: 
			__type_byte_short__ - __type_byte__
			__type_word_short__ - __type_word__
			__type_dword_short__ - __type_dword__
			__type_xor_short__ - __type_xor__
			__type_qword_short__ - __type_qword__
			__type_float_short__ - __type_float__
			__type_double_short__ - __type_double__
			__type_auto_short__ - __type_auto__
		Also you can write the number in hex (little-endian) notation:
			__hex_example____hex__ __semicolon__ 6543
		or in reverse hex (big-endian) notation:
			__hex_example____rhex__ __semicolon__ 6543
		To input additional symbols, use the "__btn_hex__" button on internal keyboard.
	4. Select the suitable data type, or choose "__type_auto__" (if you are not sure) to use as default. Other options do not matter for this type of search. Click "__search__".
	5. When finished searching, search result will be displayed in the list.
	6. Back to the game. After the value has changed, switch to __app_name__ again.
	7. Repeat group search with appropriate changes.
	8. If there are too many results, repeat steps 6 and 7.
	9. If few results are left, you can try to modify, store or freeze the value.

	If you want respect order of values then specify __colon__ twice.
	Without group size:
		195 __semicolon__ 6543 __colon____colon__
	With group size:
		195 __semicolon__ 6543 __colon____colon__ 300

	You can search in the range of values:
		190__tilde__210 __semicolon__ 6543
	Also you can search outside the range of values:
		10__tilde____tilde__4000 __semicolon__ 6543

* Others:
	* For new searches, you must clear the previous search results by selecting "__clear__" in the menu under the menu button.
	* To select another game tap on the game icon in the upper left corner.
	* To pause/resume a game - tap on the button below the icon in the upper left corner.
	* If you switch to a game while pause is active, the next time you open __app_name__ , that game will be paused automatically.
	* All frozen values are automatically added to the saved list. You can manage them under the appropriate tab.
	* All input data saves to the "__history__" list. You can use them by pressing the history button.

* Typical usage algorithms:

1) The value is not encrypted and known.
	A typical case would be for money, stars, coins, resources, etc.

	Use exact search. 

2) The value is unknown and/or encrypted.
	A typical case of an unknown value - any scale/bar. For example health, mana, nitro, etc. The changes are seen, but no numerical values are visible.
	A typical case for encrypted values would be money, stars, coins, etc., but when searching with 1 algorithm does not find anything, or change of found values does not lead to numbers changing in the game.

	Use fuzzy search.


3) The value is encrypted (__mode_hacking__).
	A typical case for encrypted values would be money, stars, coins, etc., but when searching with 1 algorithm does not find anything, or change of found values does not lead to numbers changing in the game.

	Use exact search, but always select the checkbox "__mode_hacking__". 
	Found results may have strange values due to encryption.

Algorithm 3 is much faster than 2, but it may not work for all games.

4) The value is not encrypted, known and placed near other known values.
	A typical case would be for price in store, hero stats, etc.

	Use group search for all numbers in one time.

* Hints:

	* Not all games can be modified by __app_name__. Typical list of examples:
		* data stored on a server (server-sided games)
		* the game needs persistent internet connection
		* network play with other players
		* you need to select a server to connect at the start of the game
		* you can not use some things without internet
		* game reloads data from server if you change something
	* Values that are seen are not always the same as the way they are stored. They can be different. For example on screen we see 34, but in memory the value is 34000.
	* If you can not modify a value - try modifying values which affected this value. For example, instead of changing the amount of gold, you can change the price of the thing and sell it. Or buy with a negative price.
Items (__d__) added to the saved list
There are no items to edit.

The saved list is empty.
You can add items to it from the search result list or from the memory editor.
Simply select the desired elements and choose "__save_selected__" in the menu.
Another method - begin editing a value and check the checkbox "__save_as__".
Also all frozen values are added here automatically.
The saved list is empty. There is nothing to save.
This app can be downloaded from:
Did you forget to \'__hex__\' at the end?
Enter the number of seconds to jump ahead:
__app_name__ performs a reinstallation of itself with a random name to exclude detection by games. Wait.
No space left on your device
__app_name__ can be detected when not installed. Do you really want to cancel?
The target app must be restarted to apply these changes.

For the operation of this program, your device must have root.

__app_name__ can work in limited mode without root, through a virtual environment. For example, through __vspace_list__ and many others.
Read the help for more details.

How to root any device: http://www.xda-developers.com/root/
Indent UI from top of the screen:
Path must be at least 2 characters long and starts with \'/\'.
__s__ in real time = __s__ in game time
Go back to the game, if a lot of values are found
Go back to the game, if a lot of values are found and restore the search mode on next open

After you start the application, by clicking the "__start__" button, a floating icon will appear on the screen. It can be dragged to any location.
Short tapping on the icon will open the main application interface, long tap - show a panel for changing the speed of the game.

In the main screen you can see:
	* In the upper left corner - the icon of the game. Tapping on it allows you to select another game.
	* Located in the center are bookmarks to navigate through the tabs. Tapping on the desired bookmark displays the appropriate tab.

In total there are 4 tabs: settings, search, saved list and the memory editor.
	* In the upper right corner of the interface is the closing button. Press it and the interface will be hidden, and a floating icon will appear on the screen.
	* At the bottom of the screen there is a status bar and the eye icon. If you press and hold on the eye icon, the interface becomes transparent, as long as you do not release your finger.
	* The rest of the contents on the screen depends on which tab you're on.

On each tab, except for the settings tab, there is a menu button, which contains a variety of possible actions. In it you will find the main actions for this tab.

Settings tab contains a variety of settings, help, and information about the application, and the exit button.

Search tab contains:
	* Pause button, the name of the selected game, the number of results found, and the refresh button.
	* Below is a panel with quick access icons and a menu button. All icons are duplicated on the menu, so if you do not know the meaning of the icon, it can always be viewed by clicking on the menu button (the rightmost).
	* Below those icons will be the list of search results.

Saved list tab contains:
	* Pause button, the name of the selected application, the menu button and the refresh button.
	* Below those icons will be the list of saved values.

Memory editor tab is similar to the previous, only here instead of a list of saved values it displays memory contents.

In all three lists (the search results, saved values and memory editor), you can perform a long tap on the items to show the context menu.

Daemon was killed by something.

Usually it can be:
	1. Any memory killers or optimizers. You must disable any killers or add __app_name__ the white list in its settings.
	2. __daemon_killed_oom__
	3. The memory card was unmounted, while __app_name__ stored temporary files on it. To prevent this from happening, specify a different path to the temporary files. For example, in the internal memory.
Using freeze values together with the setting __hide_from_game__ can cause lag in the game.
This translation was created by an automatic translator. You can improve it by using our collective translation service.
Disable the floating windows and restart the app
Enable the floating windows and restart the app

* Range search:
	1. Switch to __app_name__ from a game.
	2. Tap the "__search_known_value__" button in the "__search__" tab.
	3. Enter a border search range, separated by "__tilde__". For example, to search for values from 100 to 150, enter:
			100__tilde__150
		To search outside the range, you can specify the character "__tilde__" twice:
			100__tilde____tilde__150
		Alternatively, you can select the sign "__not_equal__" and specify the character "__tilde__" once.
		To input additional symbols, use the "__btn_hex__" button on internal keyboard.
	4. Select the suitable data type, or choose "__type_auto__" (if you are not sure) and other options (if needed) and click "__search__".
	5. When finished searching, search results will be displayed in the list.
	6. Back to the game. After the value has changed, switch to __app_name__ again.
	7. Repeat range search with appropriate changes.
	8. If there are too many results, repeat steps 6 and 7.
	9. If few results are left, you can try to modify, store or freeze the value.

	The boundaries of the range included in the search results. You can select the type of search "__not_equal__" to find all values that do not belong to the range.
	In this search, all values are considered signed.
Range bounds must be the same type.
Video tutorials and guides how to hack
Load the saved list from the file
Save the saved list to the file
Could not attach to process (PTRACE_ATTACH): Game is protected
Forcibly terminating a process does not give the process an opportunity to shut down cleanly and can cause data loss or system instability.

* Search for encrypted values:
	1. Switch to __app_name__ from a game.
	2. Tap the "__search_known_value__" button in the "__search__" tab.
	3. Input the number that is displayed in the game.
	4. Select the suitable data type, or choose "__type_auto__" (if you are not sure), any other options (if needed), check "__mode_hacking__" and click "__search__".
	5. When finished searching, search results will be displayed in the list.
	6. Go back to the game. After the value has changed, switch to __app_name__ again.
	7. Search for the value that changed. Checkbox "__mode_hacking__" must be checked.
	8. If there are too many results, continue to step 6.
	9. If only a few results are left, you can try to modify, store or freeze the value.
Why can not I enter a value greater than 99 or 60,000?
You found the value of __type_byte__ or __type_word__. Look for __type_dword__ to enter larger values.
How to search for fractional (__type_float__) values, without fractional part of the value?
Use the \"__type_auto__\" data type or range search. For example, for 11.345 you can search for \"10__tilde__12\".
I found the value and changed it, but in the game this change does not do anything. I\'m just trying to use it but everything comes back. What am I doing wrong?

In some games, the values are protected or encrypted. Before displaying the value, the value is decrypted into another variable. You changed it.\nSince this value is used only for display, it does not affect the game mechanics. To find the real value, rather than the displayed value, use the search for the encrypted value, or the fuzzy search.\nSometimes the value is stored on the server and synchronized each time. In this case, it can not be hacked. If the game does not work without the Internet and the previous ways did not help - most likely this is the case.

Does not find the value in game X. Or finds it, but when changed nothing happens. Or it finds it, but after it\'s changed the game crashes. How do I know what the problem is, is it the protection in the game or with __app_name__?

You must take a simple program that is guaranteed to have no protection and try it. I prefer to use "Hill Climb Racing". The game is simple, uncomplicated and does not contain any defenses. You can check the hacking of money as a direct search by number, and fuzzy search for change.
If hacking on this simple game works, then it's in that X game that you tried to hack. If not, then the problem is in __app_name__.
There is even an easier option: "Tap Counter". Install it. Run. Try to crack the number of tapes displayed on the screen. It should be very easy. If not, then the problem is in __app_name__ or its settings.
Try another version of __app_name__, try to search for a description of the error received on the forum in the relevant topic or on the Internet. In extreme cases, you can ask around in the forum.

- Try to search as the number of days, hours, minutes or seconds. The data type can be either __type_byte__, __type_word__, __type_dword__ or __type_qword__. Also you can use type __type_auto__.
	- Try to search as a number of milli- or nanoseconds. The data type is __type_dword__ or __type_qword__.
	- Try to search as unix timestamp the end date. Type - __type_dword__. It is considered as the difference between the current unix timestamp and the saved one. Search through the range search.
For example, if 30 days are written, then you are looking for 30 +/- 1 day from the current unix timestamp. The current unix timestamp can be easily viewed on the Internet.
Is it possible to hack online games?

It is possible to hack the data that is stored on the client. Anything on the server can not be hacked. In different games can be different ways. Even within a single player game, some of the data may be on the server, and some may not.

Speedhack may not work on x86 devices / emulators for games that do not have x86 libraries.\nAlso for an ART environment, speedhack does not work with Java timers. If the game uses them and you have an ART environment (usually Android 5 and above), then speedhack will not work.
__app_name__ as a whole works for me, but in some games I\'m getting the PTRACE_ATTACH error. Why and what to do about it?
This game is equipped with protection against debugging by the type of SecNeo protection. __app_name__ can not bypass this protection. Through it, you can not hack this game.
Why does the application have such a strange package name? Why is the application not updated, but every time install separately? Why does each version have a different package name?
To prevent detection by anti-cheats by package name.
In some games, when changing speed (speedhack), they stop responding to clicks. Is it game protection or program error?

This means that your speedhacking intercepts the input queue timers and it dies. It is necessary to select green checkmarks in the list of timers (in the settings of __app_name__) so that this does not happen.
Why does it issue a comma separated list of errors?
Because the delimiter of numbers is a semicolon (\"__semicolon__\").
How do I perform multiple searches at the same time?
At the moment, the application does not allow this, but you can install the application several times and run the copies together if there is enough memory and disk space.
Do I need to update the application if there is nothing significant in the changes?
It is suggested. Support is only for the latest version.
What are the symbols \"!\" and \"#\", before the process name?

This marks the traced processes.
	"#" - before the process means that it is traced by some other process.
	"!" - before the process, this process is traced some other process.
Tracing is typical for debugging or hacking. Also it is used in some protections. Typically, a process with a "#" sign is a protected process.
Why does the application after the standard installation also perform its own installation, issuing requests for root rights?

__app_name__ reinstalls itself with random data to prevent its detection by anti-cheats. Root rights are needed for \"silent\" installation and uninstallation. You can not give them out. In this case, you will have to manually respond to all requests of the system for installing applications.
What is __pkg__? Is it a trojan or a virus? Is it safe to give this application root access?
This is the base name of the __app_name__ package. Issuing root access is safe if __app_name__ is downloaded from trusted sources.

Why is __app_name__ in the list of installed applications named as a random character set? Why does this character set request root access? Why when launching __app_name__ writes that the root is not provided by __app_name__, but by some kind of abracadabra?
Because __app_name__ uses it to hide itself from detection by anti-cheats.
Why is __app_name__ of the latest versions sometimes detected by anticheats, and sometimes not?

Because __app_name__ uses different things to hide itself from detection by anti-cheats. This includes a random package name and application. At each installation they are different. Try reinstalling __app_name__ to regenerate. Perhaps after this anti-cheat will cease to see __app_name__.
Why is my game not in the application list?
Because this is a list of running applications, and your application is not running. Run it, and then select it in __app_name__.
Why can Java applications not respond to speedhack on Android 5 and above?

Because speedhack is designed for games with native libraries. Your application is not. And since Android 5 used the environment of ART, instead of Dalvik, which makes it impossible to intercept Java timers. Do not do anything. The situation will not change in the future.
I used a different speedhack / hacking tool on Android 4 and everything worked. Now I put __app_name__ on Android 5 and above, but those methods do not work. Why?

Because many things have changed and your utility would not work just like that. Do not create your illusions that this __app_name__ is so bad, but if there was that utility, then uh! There\'s no such thing.\nTo test this, install __app_name__ on Android 4, and repeat all the same actions as in that utility. As a result, you will succeed. But this does not mean that these same methods will work on Android 5 and above.\nThis fact is confirmed experimentally, repeatedly.
I\'m watching a video sample / screenshots and there are other data in the memory editor. Why?
Because the video / screenshot has a different output format. In the memory editor, press the menu - select \"__value_format__\" and configure the output as it should.
When the value is frozen, the game starts to brake or lag. Why?
Because you have in the __app_name__ settings the option to \"__hide_from_game__\" enabled. Turn it off.
How can I disable checking for new versions when I start the application?
How to rewind the time back in the application? Is it possible to make a time jump not forward, but backward?

No way. You can not. Time must always increase otherwise the game can crash. However, in the latest versions we added the possibility of a negative time jump. The result may be a hang of the game or loss of data. Be warned.
I put the icon on and it disappeared. How can I get it back?
The icon becomes transparent, but you can still click on it. Click where it was, then change the setting via the interface.
How do I search for a value if it is displayed in rounding with the game? For example, 1.85M.
Search for the search range. The range limits depend on the rounding rules. For example, \"1845000__tilde__1854999\" for standard rounding.
How do I search for a percentage value? For example, 123__percent__.

It is depends on the implementation. For each game it can all be different. For example 123__percent__. It can be __type_float__ or __type_double__ with any value. Like 1.23 or 123.0. Or it can be integer types: __type_byte__, __type_word__, __type_dword__. For example 123 or 12300. On output it can be divided on some number. 100 or 345 or anything else. For each game it can be different. You can try the most obvious cases. For 123__percent__ it can be 1.23 or 123. Type - __type_auto__. If you are lucky it can be what you need.

Try the following options (singly or in any combination):
	- Enable the "__hide_from_game__" options. Usually enough 2, 3 and 4 options.
	- Do not cancel the reinstallation __app_name__ with a random name. This is done the first time __app_name__ is run outside of virtual space.
	- Use XPrivacy or similar utilities. Look on the Internet for how to do this.
	- If you have root rights, use root from the virtual space with hidden installation of applications. Read more in the context help for the "__vspace_root__" setting.
Switch to work without __selinux__ and restart the app
Switch to work with __selinux__ and restart the app
Select the type of floating windows
Reset the type of floating windows
Enter an address to search for

* Address (mask) search:
	You can search or filter out by address with mask.

	A typical example is searching for addresses that end with a known value. For example, on "12E".

	The search parameters are the address and mask.
	In the address can be used wildcards ("__question__"). This symbol means that it can be any character from "0" to "F".
	An additional mask is formed from the address, so the leading zeros are important. Thus, the search for "0120" and the search for "120" is not the same thing.
	In the first case will be found: "00010120", "00020120", "00030120" and so on. While in the second: "00001120", "00002120", "00003120" and so on.

	You can search both for equality and for the inequality indicated by the mask. The second option can be useful for eliminating unnecessary values.

	The address and mask are entered in the hexadecimal system and without the letter "__hex__" at the end.
	The mask obtained from the address intersects with the mask that was entered by you. This is the resultant search mask.

	The following examples are equivalent, because the resulting mask has same - "FF0"
__mask_example__

* Fill (increment) values:
	It often happens that too many values are found, but they can not be changed from the game.
	In this case, the fill comes to the rescue.

	A typical example of use is the search for the price of an item, with a subsequent replacement by a negative one.

	1. Select the desired items.
	2. Select "__edit_selected__".
	3. Press the button "__more__" to display "__increments__" field, if this field is not present.
	4. In "__increments__" field, enter the increment value. Usually this is 1. And click "__yes__".
	5. The selected items will receive new values.
	6. Go to the game and see how the desired value has changed, moving across game screens.
	7. If the value has not changed, then the desired element not among selected. Return to __app_name__ and using the "__delete_selected__" -> "__revert_and_remove__", remove the selected items, returning them the previous value. Now you can try everything from the beginning with other elements, if there are any.
	8. If the value has changed, then the desired element is one of the selected elements. Remember the new value and return to __app_name__.
	9. In the list of selected items, search for the desired value. Unselect it. Delete all other values, using "__delete_selected__" -> "__revert_and_remove__", returning them the previous value.
	10. Modify the desired element value as you need.

	Typical errors are removal without revert. After that, the game can crash.

	If you find a lot of values, try to do fill in small portions to avoid crashes.
* If you always find the desired values in the same region (letters at the end of the items in the results list), you can select only these regions for a faster search.
__app_name__ searches for values very slowly and for a long time in the Android emulator on the PC.

Most likely you have virtualization disabled in the PC BIOS. Turn it on.
__app_name__ runs inside the emulator, so the speed of it work depends on the speed of the emulator.
If virtualization is enabled, but the speed is still low, check the settings of your emulator, or use another emulator.
You can also use the another tool - CheatEngine for PC - it will search faster and give you many features which are never will be in __app_name__.

* How to speed up search:
	0. If you are use an Android emulator on a PC (BlueStacks, Droid4X, Andy, NOX, Memu, AMIDuOS, Windroy, AVD, Genymotion, Koplayer, Leapdroid etc.), check that virtualization (VT Technology, Vanderpool Technology, Virtualization Technology, VTx, VT-d, VMX, Virtual Technology, Intel-VT, AMD-V, SVM, IOMMU) is enabled in the PC BIOS.
	1. If you have a lot of RAM, then you can store the data there. This greatly speeds up the search. Set the setting "__data_in_ram__" to "__yes__". In an Android emulator on a PC, this can slow down the search.
	2. If you have little RAM, the data will be stored on the memory card. Use a memory card with a high write and read speed.
	3. You can also specify the storage of data in the internal memory of the device, if it faster than the memory card. Set the setting "__set_path__" to the desired path.
	4. On search, select only the desired regions. Avoid slow ("__slow__") and dangerous ("__dangerous__") regions.
	5. You can also specify the limits of the memory range, for search, if you know them. Click the button "__more__" in the search dialog and enter the limits.
	6. Search for only the desired types of data. The data type "__type_auto__" is slow.
	7. Disable all possible "__hide_from_game__" options.
	8. Try using "__experimental__" or "__extended__" in "__memory_access__". These options can lead to unstable work.

Try clicking in the place where the icon was be. If this open __app_name__ interface, then the icon is hidden in the settings or your firmware has bugs with display the icon.
If the interface does not open, it means that __app_name__ has been unloaded from memory.
Possible reasons:

	1. There is not enough RAM on the device - __app_name__ killed by the system. Try use less RAM. In __app_name__ settings, do not specify the storage of data in RAM, do not set a large number of displayed results, unload "extra" applications from RAM, use "light weight" analogues of applications that can not be unloaded (for example, launcher). In especially bad cases, reboot the device before run __app_name__ with the game. Almost all of the firmware always has leak RAM. More time after reboot - the less available RAM.

	2. You have a process killers which kills __app_name__.

Disable the killers or add __app_name__ to its exceptions.
Killers are also all sorts of "optimizers" and "improvers" of RAM.
In general, see the description for the software, if you do not know is that the killer or not.

For FlyMe: __app_name__ for some reason after a few minutes of work unloaded - you need to allow work in the background for __app_name__ and your root manager (usually SuperSu).

You can also try to enable "__use_notification__" in __app_name__ settings. Sometimes this prevent kill __app_name__.
Another script is running. Interrupt it?
The progress dialog could not be displayed.

* XOR search:
		If you know that the value you need is encrypted with a XOR key that is always at the same distance from the value, then you can use this mode to search by the decrypted value.
	1. Switch to __app_name__ from a game.
	2. Tap the "__search_known_value__" button in the "__search__" tab.
	3. Enter a search number and XOR key distance, separated by "__mode_xor__". For example, to search for value 100 with a key on distance 8, enter:
			100__mode_xor__8
		To input additional symbols, use the "__btn_hex__" button on internal keyboard.
	4. Select the suitable data type, or choose "__type_auto__" (if you are not sure) and other options (if needed) and click "__search__".
	5. When finished searching, search results will be displayed in the list.
	6. Back to the game. After the value has changed, switch to __app_name__ again.
	7. Repeat XOR search with appropriate changes.
	8. If there are too many results, repeat steps 6 and 7.
	9. If few results are left, you can try to modify, store or freeze the value.

	You can use XOR search with range search. For example "100__tilde__150__mode_xor__8".
	Usually XOR search is slower from usual number search.
	This mode not supported in the group search or in the encrypted search.
	Distance must be from 1 to 4096 inclusive.
	You can input distance in hex form with __hex__ symbol at the end: "100__tilde__150__mode_xor__10h".
	Distance must respect values align. For example, dword must be aligned to 4-bytes boundary. This mean search "40__mode_xor__7" is useless for dword because 7 can not divide to 4.
	Combine this mode with another can cause problems on the bad firmware. For example, if you make usual search and after that refine in XOR mode this can cause hang or reboot your device. This must not happen if the first search will be in this mode.
	A fuzzy search of this mode is not supported. You can combine it but fuzzy work as usual and do not respect your XOR key with distance.
	Sign for values is respected. It is mean you can search value not equal, greater, smaller and so on.
	In this search, all values are considered signed.
What\'s the difference with the HW/SW versions? And which one should I use?
Perhaps this script needs the latest version of __app_name__. Try to update to the latest version.
Enter a text to search for, up to __d__ bytes in length. For example
Do you want to replace them?
Enter the text to replace \"__s__\". The length is no more than __d__ bytes.
On this firmware, you must allow working __app_name__ in the background, in the system settings.
You need a newer version of __app_name__ to run this script. At least version __s__. You have version __s__.

* __help_hex_colors_title__:\nIf the hex representation is not white, but color, then most likely in this value is the pointer. The color indicates which type of memory this pointer refers to.\nThis can be __executable__ memory, __writable__ or __read_only__. In rare cases, it can be __writable_and_executable__ memory.
An error occurred while extracting the help:
Try using the version of __app_name__ with support for older ARM processors:

* __help_add_to_value_title__:
If this option is checked, the entered value will be added to the existing value, and not replace it. For example, the element has a value of 5, and you entered 7, in the value field. If this option is checked, the element will get a new value of 5 + 7, equal to 12. If not checked, then 7.
You can enter both positive and negative values.
This option is incompatible with the freezing of the value.
Check the architecture of the game libraries:

The game is installed with ARM libraries, although in apk there are x86 libraries.\nSpeedhack and everything connected with it, will not work.\n\"__skip__\" disables these checks. You can turn it back on in the settings.
You can install the .apk file manually. It is located at the following path:
Saves the list in text format, convenient for human read. Loading of this format is not supported.
Searches for values that can be pointers to the desired address. You can specify a maximum limit for a possible offset.
The maximum limit for a possible offset.

* __help_memory_filter_title__:
You can specify a filter in the memory editor. Only those results that match the filter will be displayed.
The filter supports wildcards:
	__caret__ - the start of the data,
	__dollar__ - the end of the data,
	__asterisk__ - any number of any characters,
	__question__ - the one any character.
For example, to find the number 42, you need to enter __caret__42__dollar__.
How to get around protection in games

Some games are equipped with protection against memory editors. You can try the following:
	1. "__kill_game__", and then "__restart_game_without_protection__".
	2. "__disable_protection__", and then run (or restart) the game.
	3. Use all or part of the items in "__hide_from_game__". All of them slow down the search.
	4. Search when the game is minimized.
	5. Search when the game is paused.
	6. Kill or pause some of the protective processes of the game, if the game has several processes.
	7. Use XPrivacy or similar tools to bypass protection.
	8. Use the modification of the game without protection.
Disable protection for all applications (until reboot)
If you run the game before disabling protection, you must restart it.
This application does not support the MIPS architecture.

The unrandomizer is a feature designed to replace certain functions commonly used in games to produce random values. At a very simple level then, for an example, there may be a game that uses a randomizer function to determine the player's reward. Enabling the unrandomizer would then cause __app_name__ to try to replace this random value with the specified value in order to change the result (for example, to get a certain reward).

The unrandomizer won't work in all cases. It will only handle functions that it knows about. In most cases and for most users, this will not be used, as it's more of an advanced/experimental feature.

Some functions return __type_qword__, some - __type_double__ (rarely used).

__type_double__ values must be in the range from 0 to 1. However, you can specify any values there, because __app_name__ will correct all the incorrect variants automatically.

You can also specify an increment step. In this case, each subsequent result will increase by this value. For example, for increment 1 and the initial value 0, instead of a random value, 0, 1, 2, 3, ... will be returned.
This option can be useful for a complete search of all possible random results.

You can specify which functions to modify in the __app_name__ settings.
How many first results to replace?

This option will try to restart the game without protection.
However, many firmware contains bugs that can cause the device to reboot.
If this happens, do not try to start the game again, as this will restart the device again.
Just run __app_name__ - it will clear the bad settings.
Also you can try to reboot the device in a regular way, this too should fix the problem.

If the reboot has occurred - do not use this function, it will not work on your firmware.

This option will try to patch the system (temporarily) so that all applications run without protection.
The changes will apply only to new applications starts. Therefore, the target application, after a successful patch, needs to be restarted.
To cancel the patch, just restart the device.
Perhaps there will be various bugs in the system, after this patch.

If the patch is successful, it is not recommended to run this function again, before rebooting the device.

If you have problems, do not use this function.
All entries, including pinned, will be deleted.
This feature requires root access. Currently __app_name__ is running in a virtual environment mode without root access.

__app_name__ can work without root, through a virtual environment. For example, through __vspace_list__ and many others.
__app_name__ must be installed in a virtual environment. The game must also be installed there.
Not all features of the program are supported, but most functions should work.

You will not be able to crack games that are not installed in a virtual environment.

The first launch of __app_name__, which performs a reinstallation with a random package name, must not be performed through a virtual environment.
Then you can add __app_name__ to the virtual environment and use.
Why my game is not in the process list?

The fractional part of a real number "x" is the excess beyond that number's integer part.
For a number its fractional part hence corresponds to the digits appearing after the radix point.

For example:

You can filter the search results.
You can specify:
	- the limits for the address,
	- the limits for the value,
	- the required data types,
	- the desired fractional part of the number,
	- the desired type of pointer.
You can combine any filters with each other.
You can also disable one or any of the filters at any time.

However, if a large amount of data is found, the filters can slow down the results.

A filter on the fractional part of a number can be either by equality or by inequality.
For integer types, the fractional part is zero. If you do not need integer types, exclude them using a type filter.

Filter by pointer type allows to leave in the output only those values that can be a pointer to the desired type of memory.
Possible memory types correspond to the colors in the memory editor:
This can be __executable__ memory, __writable__ or __read_only__. In rare cases, it can be __writable_and_executable__ memory.

The pseudo-type "no pointer" corresponds to all types of data that can not be pointers, and also to those values of pointers that point to unallocated memory.

The memory card was unmounted, while __app_name__ stored temporary files on it. This led to the forced closure of the daemon.

To prevent this from happening, specify a different path to the temporary files. For example, in the internal memory.
There should be enough free space.
On some firmware, for example Lenovo, the alternative mode can work faster.\n\nNormally, you do not need to change this option.
In the interface, some elements are marked with a special icon, at the top right, with the symbol __i__ in the circle.\nPressing or long pressing, on this element, shows help on this element.

On some firmware, the __waitpid__ system call does not work as expected. This is usually due to incorrect SeLinux settings.\nThis leads to an endless search in __app_name__.\nYou can try using an alternative mode. Maybe this will work.
Too small values can cause a high load on the processor and lags in the game.
The interval through which __app_name__ overwrites the values in the game memory.
The interval through which __app_name__ updates the values in the saved list.
The size of the icons in the __app_name__ interface.\nDoes not apply to a floating icon.
Can help solve problems when copying numbers from different places, if special characters are copied along with the numbers.

Use of notifications in the system notification area of Android.
On some firmware, this increases the priority of the application and prevents it from closing.
Also you can use the notification to go to __app_name__ or the game by clicking on it.
This can be useful if you can not click on the floating icon.
System sounds when you press buttons and items.\nOn some firmware, sounds cause the __app_name__ to crash.
The internal keyboard takes up less memory, loads faster and is optimized for entering numbers.
Hints from an external keyboard.\nOn some firmware, hints cause the __app_name__ to crash.
Automatically puts the game on pause when opening the __app_name__ interface.
__app_name__ was forcibly closed by the application \"__s__\". To avoid this, delete this application or configure it so that it does not terminate the __app_name__.

You have installed applications that can forcibly close __app_name__:
__s__
To avoid this, delete them, or configure them so that they do not terminate the __app_name__.

If you do not experience problems with the unexpected closing of the __app_name__, then no action is required, and you can ignore this message.

__app_name__ will be restarted with recording __logcat__ into the file:
"__s__".

To end the recording, exit from __app_name__.
After that, you can send the log file to where you need it.
You need to uninstall the installer, otherwise you will have 4 icons, not 2. Also games will detect it.

__app_name__ uses the standard package name. Games can detect it.

To fix this - run __app_name__ outside of the virtual space as a normal application.
After the installation is completed, add a copy of __app_name__ with a random name to the virtual space.

You need to install the virtual space application. Such as: __vspace_list__.

It is recommended to install optimized versions from the official __app_name__ site.

Then you must add __app_name__ and your game to this virtual space.

After that you must run the game and __app_name__ in the virtual space.
Another debugger or cracker is connected to the game.
Insufficient permissions. Usually due to incorrectly configured __selinux__. Disable or configure __selinux__.
Bad kernel or firmware. Try another firmware or other device.
On your firmware, the \"no root\" mode does not work. Try another firmware or other device or get root.

Indicates what to do after disabling the protection connection:
	__mode_restore__ - restore connection
	__mode_nothing__ - do nothing (usually the protection itself restores the connection)
	__mode_freeze__ - freeze the thread (as in previous versions)

If you restore the protection, or allow the protection to do it yourself, the freezing of values in __app_name__ can cause lags due to the permanent connection / disabling of protection.
You are using the \"__s__\" emulator. It is slow and contains errors. Because of this, the search can be very slow.\n\nWe recommend using other emulators:

__app_name__ is installed on a memory card that is used as "Internal storage".
This can interfere with the operation of virtual spaces.
If this happens, install __app_name__ in the internal memory of the device.
You may need to reformat the memory card as "Portable storage".

The game must be running to select it.

If the game is running, but still it is not in the list, then maybe you have problems with the root, or firmware.
Try another root or other firmware.

The game must be launched in the virtual space so that it can be selected.

You can not select a game if it is not running in the virtual space.

If the game is running in the virtual space, but still it is not in the list, try another virtual space, another firmware, another device or get a root.

Daemon was killed by the system due to lack of memory.

Set "__data_in_ram__" to "__no__".
Use hardware acceleration of the interface, if possible.
Do not use large apps.
Disable all other unused apps.
Try to use __app_name__ after rebooting your device.
This will replace the current search results. Continue?

The script wants to access the Internet. If you allow access, the script will be able to steal your logins, passwords, saves and other data from the game. Also it will be able to steal your other scripts, or download viruses to your device.\n\nAllow the script access to the Internet?

To properly run __app_name__ on your firmware, you need to allow usage popup windows in system settings for __s__.

The popup does not show on Huawei phones by default. You will need to allow __s__ to show popup. Please open the Phone Manager, then Dropzone Management and please make sure it is turned on for __s__.

The Popup also does not show on some Xiaomi phones. You need to head to Settings -> Installed apps -> __s__ -> Other permissions -> Display pop-up window.

Also, other phones may have different procedures to allow apps to show Popup windows. All such apps are usually affected this way, for example the Google Translator app with the Tap To Translate functionality. Therefore, you can simply google "YOUR_PHONE tap to translate" to find the solution. For example, for Xiaomi Redmi devices the solution is as follows:

Prevent unloading __app_name__ from memory (force close).

Each subsequent level is more powerful than the previous one and includes it in itself.

The third level can cause problems (hang toast, reboots or crashes) on some firmware and emulators.

Without root only the third level works.
This data type is only for integer values and does not support fractional values. Use __type_float__ or __type_double__ for fractional values.
Also you can use __app_name__ without floating windows:
You can use __any__ in an ordered group search along with the minimum size to specify any value of that type at the desired position.

* __help_scripts_title__:
__app_name__ supports the execution of scripts written in Lua.

On the search tab, click the menu and select "__execute_script__".
Here you can select the script file for execution, see the existing __app_name__ API methods, or go to the official site to read help on the API or download ready scripts.
Also, you can extract help from the API on the memory card to read it without the Internet.

If you want to write your own script, then you need an external text file editor.
__app_name__ does not allow editing scripts, it only executes them.

Read API help for more details.
If after clicking the \"__start__\" button you do not see a floating icon:

You ran __app_name__ in a virtual space that supports 64-bit processes.

__app_name__ will not see 32-bit processes running in this space.

Install the second copy of __app_name__ in the "__for32__" mode if you want to work in __app_name__ with 32-bit processes.
There is a file with that name
The previous app shutdown was unexpected.\n\nYou can raise the \"__prevent_unload__\" configuration level to avoid this in the future.
For hack 32-bit games in 64-bit virtual space
For hack 64-bit games in 64-bit virtual space
Reset all ignore for all dialogs
Could not allocate memory page. Error
The installation was successful.\nRun the installed copy manually so that it can migrate your data.\nAfter that, the installer can be removed.
Another record is running. Stop it?

You are using a non-optimized version of the virtual space (\"__s__\").\nTo fix this error, remove the non-optimized version of \"__s__\" and install one of the optimized virtual spaces from the official site __app_name__.\nClick \"__fix_it__\" to go to the selection of optimized virtual spaces.
Free space or specify a different path for temporary files by clicking \"__fix_it__\".
Reading memory by all available methods, if it could not be read.\nMay slow down memory readings.
Failed to change memory through ptrace.
Some features, speedhack and everything related to it, will not work. Writing to read-only memory (including code) is not possible.
The absolute majority of features will not work.

The typical reason is a broken kernel in the firmware.
Try changing the firmware. If you recently updated it - install the previous version.

If this does not work, change the device.
This feature cannot work if the fourth item is selected in \"__hide_from_game__\".
This feature cannot work without ptrace, which does not work on this firmware.
This feature cannot work due to ptrace protection in the game.
Check that the __app_name__ is installed correctly.
If __app_name__ is installed on external storage (SD card), return it to internal memory
The offset is not a multiple of __d__.
The resulting address is not a multiple of __d__.
__d__ elements will change its type to correct the impossible alignment.

Unable to reinstall __app_name__ with random name.

__app_name__ can be easily detected by games.

You can record __logcat__, during installation, and contact the official forum for help.

Rejection reason:
Empty input field: no number to convert
You must select an integer to convert.
Need root to hack this game.

The problem of virtual space or firmware or device.

Try:
	- use another virtual space
	- use another firmware
	- get root
	- use another device

The problem of firmware or root or device.

Try:
	- use another firmware
	- use another root
	- use another device

Using root from virtual space can help hide the fact that there is a __app_name__ on the device.

It does not work on all firmware and not on all virtual spaces.

Virtual space, firmware and root should allow working with root from virtual space.
The virtual space should allow you to install applications inward without installing them into the system.

Not all virtual spaces can provide this.

Not on all firmware this mode will work. And not with all root.

Since the root rights will be granted to the entire virtual space, you should not install anything else in it. Otherwise, any application from the virtual space can use root rights with unpredictable consequences.

The game must be installed outside the virtual space.

__app_name__ should be installed only inside the virtual space, without installing it into the system.
How to do this - see the help of a particular virtual space.

For example, for __vexp__, you can clone __app_name__ from the system in __vexp__, and then delete __app_name__ from the system.
9. Use root in virtual spaces that allow to install applications into yourself without installing into the system.
\'__s__\' - decimal separator\n\'__s__\' - thousands separator\n\'__semicolon__\' - number separator in group search

In some applications, (for example, such as console emulators), the value in memory may not respect alignment, or be presented in big-endian byte order.

Such value can not be found through a normal search.

To find them - use a special script from the official __app_name__ website:

Almost in each field for entering numbers, you can enter a mathematical formula, after the equal sign, for example, in the search field enter '= 2 + 2 * 3', and the program will search for the result of the formula, that is, '8'.
The formula is a Lua expression. You can use all the available power of Lua in it.

All arithmetic operators Lua 5.3 are admissible - addition (+), subtraction (-), multiplication (*), division (/), division remainder (%), exponentiation (^), integer division (//), and so on.
Also available are bit operators - AND (&), OR (|), Exclusive OR (binary ~), NOT (unary ~), Shift to the right (>>), Shift to the left (<<). The order of action can be given by brackets.
Read the Lua manual for a complete listing of available operations.

It is useful if you know how the value is coded in the game. For example, 29 rounds in one game can be found as '= 29 ~ 444444'. This is equivalent to 29 XOR 444444. In the video sample, the price was found 3888 through XOR with 444444, as '= 444444 ~ 3888'. After this, the replacement was made to '= 444444 ~ 1', which set the price to '1'. In another game, 125 gold is like '= 125 * 10'.

Not all script functions are available in the formula calculator. Some functions are not available. If you do not have enough of them - write your script, instead of using a formula calculator, it is intended only for simple calculations. Available: part of the basic functions, 'bit32', 'table', 'string', 'utf8', 'math', part of the 'os' package. Packages 'debug', 'package', 'gg', 'io' are not available.

When you write '= 2 + 2 * 3', the keyword 'return' will be added before the formula: 'return 2 + 2 * 3'. However, you can specify this word yourself in a formula if you need a complex formula.
For example, '= a = 5; b = 7; return a * 4 + b * 8' will be executed as 'a = 5; b = 7; return a * 4 + b * 8'

The result of the formula calculator is a text string. The string can be any valid string for this input field.
For example, it may be a string for a group search or a range search. '= a = 5; b = 7; return a .. ";" .. b .. ":: 100"' is equivalent to '5; 7 :: 100'.

Formula calculator does not work in scripts, there you can directly use Lua.
When writing a script, the result of the formula calculation will be written to the file, not the formula itself.

If the formula is incorrect, you will receive an error message when you submit the form.

When calculating, you must take into account the fact that calculations are carried out in the type that the first operand specifies. If it is an integer, then the result will be an integer.
For example, '= 3/2' is '1', and '= 3.0 / 2' is '1.5'.

Regardless of the chosen locale, the formulas work in the English locale. This means that the decimal separator is always a dot ('.').

Two launch icons. The first, with software acceleration - "__app_short_name_sw__", the second, with hardware acceleration - "__app_short_name_hw__". Now it will always be so.
On some firmwares only one version works, on normal ones both.
It is better to use software acceleration, it works less smoothly, but consumes less memory.

You can hide one of the icons in the application settings: "__hide_icons__".

Hardware or software interface acceleration __app_name__.
Depending on the game and firmware, some of the options can cause problems.
If both work, use software acceleration because it consumes less memory.

This option is separate from the launch icons and only affects the display of the __app_name__ interface.
Launch icons are responsible for the type of acceleration of the start screen.

Hardware acceleration consumes much more memory, but it works more smoothly. If you have little RAM, always use software acceleration.

If hardware acceleration causes errors, __app_name__ can switch to software acceleration.
Allows you to display more information in the item lists, but it can be more difficult to click on the desired item.

This icon was used to the current launch.

You cannot hide the icon that was used to the current launch.

Restart the application through another icon to be able to hide this icon.
The icon will be hidden for __d__ seconds so that you can take a screenshot.\n\nAfter that, the icon will be visible again.
Use visible data type in memory editor:
Do not ask for the data type in the memory editor if only one data type is displayed.
In your locale, the number \"__s__\" looks like \"__s__\". If this is not what you need - select a different locale (or language) on the start screen.
Also, in the __app_name__ settings, you can override the locale for formatting numbers.

To search for text, you must specify modifiers in the usual search dialog.

The most commonly used encodings are __utf8__ and __utf16le__.
Typically, __utf16le__ is used in __java__, and __utf8__ is used in __c__.

	__utf16le__ is a two-byte encoding. Each character is always encoded in two bytes, so it is represented via __type_word__.
	__utf8__ - encoding with variable byte size per character. Represented via __type_byte__.
	__hex_input__ and its variants are always represented via __type_byte__.

The game can use any of the encodings.
If you need a specific set of bytes - use the __hex_input__ mode.

Available prefixes:
__text_prefixes__

For "__hex_mixed__" mode, you can specify __utf8__ text between __apos__ characters and __utf16le__ text between __quot__ characters.

For example:
__text_examples__

The prefix character must be the first character in the search text.

The result of the search will be a __type_byte__ sequence, or a __type_word__ sequence, for the case of __utf16le__.

The results can be changed - select all and click "__edit_selected__".

In the value field, enter the new value, in the same format as for the search string.

You can use the fill buttons. Click "__more__" if they are not visible.

The value will be set for each group of continuously following items.
If there is a gap between the elements, it will be a new group.

If you specify more bytes than needed, then only a part of them will be used.
If you specify less than needed, then the rest of the group will be filled with zero bytes.

When replacing text, try to specify text with the same number of bytes as the original to avoid problems.
It works much faster than normal freezing, but not for all memory.\n\nIf the freeze does not work, disable this option.
If you are using __magisk_hide__ - disable it before applying the patch. After successfully applying the patch, you can turn it back on.\nIf you do not, the patch will not be successful.

How to hide __app_name__ from the game in virtual space

	1. Install the virtual space, which allows the hidden installation of applications, without installing them in the system. For example, __vspace_hide__.
	2. Install __app_name__ into the virtual space.
	3. In __app_name__, turn on the "__vspace_root__" mode.
	4. Grant root rights to virtual space. If you do not able to grant root rights - try another virtual space, other root rights, different firmware or change the device.
	5. Remove __app_name__ from the system.
	6. Work with __app_name__ from virtual space. 

For avoid detect during the search:
	Select items 2, 3 and 4 in "__hide_from_game__" setting. 

A typical solution (requires root, without root you won’t succeed):
	1. Use root from virtual space. Read the help for the setting "__vspace_root__".
	2. Use items 2, 3 and 4 in the "__hide_from_game__" setting.
You can set \"__data_in_ram__\" to \"__no__\" to avoid this in the future.
It looks like you are trying to execute __s__ as a script.
But in this game is no x86 libraries, so maybe it will not work.
Text messages (toasts) remain on the screen, or a dark circle is visible at the bottom of the screen when __app_name__ is working. How to fix it?

Using the third level \"__prevent_unload__\" on some firmware leads to the fact that text messages (toasts) remain on the screen, or a dark circle is visible (empty message). If this happens, do not use the third level.
In future versions of __app_name__, this script cannot be run.\nWrite about this to the author of the script.
Try reinstalling the game with x86 libraries?\n\nThe game may stop working after that. If this happens, reinstall the game from the apk located in __data_app__.

__app_name__ has a built-in disassembler / assembler for Lua scripts.

You can select the "__debug_disassemble__" option in the script launch window, after clicking the "__more__" button.

The resulting __ext_lasm__ file can be edited and selected in the script launch window. The result will be a binary __ext_lua__ file, with the changes made.

Read the script API help for more details.
How to hide __app_name__ from the game
These values cannot be found through a group search, they are too far apart: __d__ bytes. The maximum distance is __d__ bytes.
You selected the first item. Freezing values can cause severe lags in the game.
You selected the fourth item. Some features will not work: __speedhack__, __unrandomizer__, __allocate_page__.

A problem was detected with this 64-bit virtual space.

To fix it:

1. Install the __fixer__ application. (From this dialog, either manually along the path: 
__s__
).

2. Add __fixer__ to the virtual space.

3. Run __fixer__ in virtual space.

4. Remove __fixer__.

5. Restart the __app_name__ in virtual space.
